title: SVG city marker overlay on map
description: Add an SVG element positioned absolutely over the map image that renders a small circle marker for each city at its pixel coordinates. The SVG dimensions must match the map image exactly. Each marker: small filled circle, hover tooltip showing city name, highlighted state (larger, different colour) when that city is selected. Markers are clickable and call onSelectCity. Use React to render the SVG markers from the city data array.

title: City list React component in sidebar
description: Build the city list sidebar in index.html as a React component. Render all cities from the JS data array in a scrollable list. Each city name is a clickable element. Style to be clean and readable - grouped alphabetically. Selected city gets a highlighted state (background colour change). Component uses shared selectedCityId state and an onSelectCity callback. The list should auto-scroll to bring the selected city into view when selection changes from the map side.

title: Add pixel coordinates to city data
description: Extend the city JS array in index.html with pixel x/y coordinates for each city marker position on the map image. Starting point: use the grid reference letters/numbers to calculate approximate pixel positions based on the image dimensions and grid cell size. Then open the page in a browser and manually adjust each coordinate by visual inspection. Add a dev helper mode that shows a click-to-log coordinate tool to make positioning easier. Document the coordinate origin (top-left of image) and that coords are in natural image pixels.

title: Bidirectional click interaction
description: Wire up the two-way interaction using shared state in the top-level App component. Clicking a city in the list: sets selectedCityId, the map panel scrolls to bring that city marker into view (scrollIntoView or manual scroll to marker position). Clicking a marker on the map: sets selectedCityId, the city list scrolls to bring that entry into view. Both panels stay in sync. Verify the scroll behaviour works correctly when opened via file:// in the browser.

title: Display map image in map panel
description: Wire the extracted map image (src/main/resources/map_area_only.png) into the map panel of web/index.html. The image currently includes decorative elements: dragon border, compass rose, fleur-de-lis, and the lettered/numbered grid border. Decision needed: crop to just the inner map area, or keep the grid border (useful for verifying city coordinate positions). Recommend keeping the grid border for now and cropping later once coordinates are tuned. Use relative img src - works correctly with file:// paths. Map in a scrollable container (overflow: auto). Verify in browser opened via file://.

title: Build city list - manual transcription from PDFs
description: Create the city data as a JS array embedded in web/index.html. Source: darklands_map.pdf (city index with grid refs) and darklands_cluebook.pdf pages 28-31 (extended list). Both are low-quality scans - OCR will be imperfect so plan for manual review and correction. Each city object needs at minimum: id (slug), name (string), gridRef (e.g. C4). Pixel coordinates are a separate task. Aim to get the complete list accurate before moving on - this data underpins everything else. Darklands has roughly 50-100 named locations.
close_reason: Done. cities.csv provided by user at src/main/resources/cities.csv - 90 cities with name, map grid ref, size, cathedral, university, and all shop quality data. No PDF parsing needed.

title: Extract map image from darklands_map.pdf
description: Use pdfimages (poppler-utils in WSL) to extract the raster map image from src/main/resources/darklands_map.pdf. The PDF is a scanned document so the map is an embedded raster. Goal: get the central map graphic as a PNG or JPG saved to web/map.jpg. The PDF also has city index columns - if they are separate image objects, extract only the map. If the whole page is one image, crop out the city index columns using ImageMagick. Document the commands used.
close_reason: Already done. map_area_only.png and map_city_index.png exist in src/main/resources/. The map image still has the decorative border, dragon, and compass rose - cropping is a separate decision addressed in hq-bau.

title: Create single-file HTML shell with two-panel layout
description: Create web/index.html as the foundation for the Darklands map viewer. No build step or server - the file is opened directly via file:// in a browser. Load React and ReactDOM from CDN (unpkg or jsdelivr), use Babel standalone for in-browser JSX. Structure: two-panel flex layout - left sidebar (~280px) for city list, right panel (flex-grow) for map. Include placeholder text in each panel. Add a basic CSS reset and the panel layout styles inline in a style tag. City data will be a JS array defined directly in a script tag in the HTML - no fetch() calls needed.

title: Map zoom and pan controls (future)
description: Backlog: add mouse-wheel zoom and drag-to-pan on the map panel. City marker coordinates must remain accurate after zoom/pan - SVG overlay must scale with the image. Consider CSS transform-based zoom on the image+SVG wrapper. Deferred until core features are stable.

title: City detail panel (future)
description: Backlog: when a city is selected, show a detail panel with city information from the cluebook data. Deferred until core map+list interaction is complete and the cluebook data (pages 28-31) has been digitised into the city data structure. No implementation yet - placeholder issue to capture the requirement.

title: Test beads installation
description: Verify that bd commands work correctly from Claude Code on Windows via WSL. This is a smoke-test task created to confirm the beads workflow is operational.
